turns.content: Install the Grafana operator from OperatorHub in namespace grafana, create a Grafana instance, and configure a datasource pointing to OpenShift Prometheus at https://thanos-querier.openshift-monitoring.svc:9091

I'll help you automate the installation of the Grafana operator, create a Grafana instance, and configure the Prometheus datasource. This playbook will:

1. Create the grafana namespace
2. Create an OperatorGroup for the namespace
3. Install the Grafana operator via Subscription
4. Wait for the operator to be ready
5. Create a Grafana instance
6. Configure a GrafanaDataSource pointing to OpenShift's Thanos Querier

Here's the Ansible playbook:

```yaml
---
- name: Install and configure Grafana operator with Prometheus datasource
  hosts: localhost
  connection: local
  gather_facts: false
  vars:
    grafana_namespace: grafana
    prometheus_url: https://thanos-querier.openshift-monitoring.svc:9091
    
  tasks:
    - name: Create grafana namespace
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        name: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        api_version: v1
        kind: Namespace
        state: present

    - name: Create OperatorGroup for Grafana operator
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1
          kind: OperatorGroup
          metadata:
            name: grafana-operatorgroup
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
          spec:
            targetNamespaces:
              - "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        state: present

    - name: Create Subscription for Grafana operator
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: operators.coreos.com/v1alpha1
          kind: Subscription
          metadata:
            name: grafana-operator
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
          spec:
            channel: v5
            name: grafana-operator
            source: community-operators
            sourceNamespace: openshift-marketplace
            installPlanApproval: Automatic
        state: present

    - name: Wait for Grafana operator to be ready
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: apps/v1
        kind: Deployment
        name: grafana-operator-controller-manager
        namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        wait_condition:
          type: Available
          status: "True"
        wait_timeout: 300

    - name: Create Grafana instance
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
          kind: Grafana
          metadata:
            name: grafana-instance
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
            labels:
              dashboards: "grafana-instance"
          spec:
            config:
              log:
                mode: "console"
              auth:
                disable_login_form: false
              security:
                admin_user: admin
                admin_password: admin123
            route:
              spec:
                to:
                  kind: Service
                  name: grafana-instance-service
                port:
                  targetPort: grafana-http
        state: present

    - name: Wait for Grafana instance to be ready
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
        kind: Grafana
        name: grafana-instance
        namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        wait_condition:
          type: Ready
          status: "True"
        wait_timeout: 300

    - name: Create service account for Prometheus access
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: v1
          kind: ServiceAccount
          metadata:
            name: grafana-prometheus-sa
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        state: present

    - name: Create ClusterRoleBinding for Prometheus metrics access
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: rbac.authorization.k8s.io/v1
          kind: ClusterRoleBinding
          metadata:
            name: grafana-prometheus-binding
          roleRef:
            apiGroup: rbac.authorization.k8s.io
            kind: ClusterRole
            name: cluster-monitoring-view
          subjects:
            - kind: ServiceAccount
              name: grafana-prometheus-sa
              namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        state: present

    - name: Get service account token secret
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: v1
        kind: Secret
        namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        label_selectors:
          - "kubernetes.io/service-account.name=grafana-prometheus-sa"
      register: sa_secrets

    - name: Create token secret if it doesn't exist
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: v1
          kind: Secret
          metadata:
            name: grafana-prometheus-sa-token
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
            annotations:
              kubernetes.io/service-account.name: grafana-prometheus-sa
          type: kubernetes.io/service-account-token
        state: present
      when: sa_secrets.resources | length == 0

    - name: Get the service account token
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: v1
        kind: Secret
        name: grafana-prometheus-sa-token
        namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
      register: token_secret

    - name: Create Prometheus datasource
      kubernetes.core.k8s:
        definition:
          apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
          kind: GrafanaDataSource
          metadata:
            name: prometheus-datasource
            namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
          spec:
            instanceSelector:
              matchLabels:
                dashboards: "grafana-instance"
            datasource:
              name: Prometheus
              type: prometheus
              access: proxy
              url: "{{ prometheus_url }}"
              isDefault: true
              jsonData:
                httpHeaderName1: "Authorization"
                tlsSkipVerify: true
              secureJsonData:
                httpHeaderValue1: "Bearer {{ token_secret.resources[0].data.token | b64decode }}"
        state: present

    - name: Get Grafana route
      kubernetes.core.k8s_info:
        api_version: route.openshift.io/v1
        kind: Route
        namespace: "{{ grafana_namespace }}"
        label_selectors:
          - "app=grafana-instance"
      register: grafana_route

    - name: Display access information
      debug:
        msg:
          - "Grafana installation completed successfully!"
          - "Grafana URL: https://{{ grafana_route.resources[0].spec.host if grafana_route.resources else 'Route not found' }}"
          - "Default credentials: admin / admin123"
          - "Prometheus datasource configured and ready"
```

This playbook will:

**Infrastructure Setup:**
- Create the `grafana` namespace
- Set up the OperatorGroup for namespace-scoped installation
- Install the Grafana operator from the community-operators catalog

**Grafana Configuration:**
- Deploy a Grafana instance with basic authentication
- Create an OpenShift route for external access
- Wait for all components to be ready

**Prometheus Integration:**
- Create a service account with cluster monitoring permissions
- Generate a service account token for authentication
- Configure a GrafanaDataSource pointing to the Thanos Querier endpoint
- Use bearer token authentication for secure access

**Security Notes:**
- The service account gets `cluster-monitoring-view` permissions to access Prometheus metrics
- TLS verification is disabled for the internal service connection
- Default admin credentials are set (change these in production)

Run with: `ansible-playbook grafana-setup.yml`

The playbook will output the Grafana URL and credentials when complete. You'll be able to access Grafana and immediately start querying OpenShift metrics through the configured Prometheus datasource.
